title: The Horizon Where Tides Read Backward

features.name: the-tide-line-that-reads
features.desc-on-touch: where water meets sand, letters appear in the foam; they spell backward but make sense if you remember tomorrow

features.name: salt-memory-stone
features.desc-on-touch: a rock that holds the taste of every high tide it has witnessed; each salt crust is a year; you can read them only by tasting, and tasting is forgetting

features.name: the-wave-that-returns-instead-of-leaves
features.desc-on-touch: watch the water: it comes in, goes out, comes in again—but the motion is reading something written on the beach before the tide arrived

features.name: the-grudge-horizon
features.desc-on-touch: far out where water meets sky, the salt keeps its anger; the horizon itself is a scar; it's been holding that grudge longer than the archive has existed

features.name: memory-made-tide
features.desc-on-touch: you wade into water that smells like every time you've been here before; the tide is made of your own returns

inscription: the tide erases and returns what it erases; memory learns the rhythm and becomes tidal itself

file.name: Tide_Recordings_Backward_Sequence_1994-2024.wav
file.note: ocean sounds from a coastal station played in reverse, but listen closely: the waves spell things; the gulls comment in order; checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable

exits.label: back to the shelf where memory still knows its shape
into open water where tide becomes entirely memory
along the coast where other horizons wait to be read

reverie.title: REVERIE — where you both stood in the tide
narr: she walked into the water and you followed
the tide came in around your knees like old friends
she pointed at something the waves were reading
you couldn't see it but her hand made the shape
when she turned, the water behind her held her footprints
